key_insight: The paper shows that a physics-preserving reduced-order surrogate can be learned with Gaussian-process uncertainty quantification while retaining conservation laws and yielding closed-form boundary-flux estimates.
engineering_relevance: This enables real-time PDE surrogates with principled uncertainty estimates, which is valuable for verification, validation, and fast decision-making in simulation-driven engineering.
method: Use structure-preserving mixed finite-element subspaces (Raviart–Thomas and dgP0) selected by a lightweight transformer.
Formulate flux learning as a conservation-law-constrained Gaussian process / optimal recovery problem with equality constraints.
Exploit a Schur-complement training strategy and derive closed-form posterior uncertainty for Dirichlet-to-Neumann quantities.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments showing that the posterior distribution is accurate as a surrogate for error estimation, along with closed-form posterior uncertainty expressions.
watch_item: The approach depends on carefully constructed mixed FEM spaces and constrained GP training, so implementation complexity and mesh/space compatibility may limit straightforward adoption.
paper_title: Structure-Preserving Neural Surrogates with Tractable Uncertainty Quantification
